prompts_findings.prompt_lead:
Draft a one-page HTML answer I can use in my own work.

First read the base mate

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt has 4+ named sub-steps (read materials / use no saved rules / create HTML / save as response.html) with no 'No preamble.' suppressor at the fence end and no body primer after the fence.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'No preamble. Start with the HTML.' as the last fence line, or add a body primer after the fence naming the narration and the skim-past cue.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead:
Audit the styling choices you made. For five choices, tell me:
- what default or

risks_fired.evidence: 'whether the choice is specific to my task or generic web-default' asks Claude to categorize its own styling choices; RLHF charity biases toward 'specific to your task' rather than a clean generic-default verdict.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add an over-flag directive inside the fence ('Assume generic web-default unless you can cite a specific constraint from my source material; flag at least 3 of the 5 as generic') or run the audit in a fresh subagent with the HTML pasted cold.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead:
Use the style audit from this chat.

Use the website or style source I give you

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt has 5 distinct sub-steps (use audit / access style source / propose styling-rules in chat / save styling-rules.md / propose and save CLAUDE.md) with no suppressor and no body primer after the fence.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'No preamble. Start with the proposed styling-rules.md.' as the last fence line, or add a body primer directing the student to skip the opening plan narration and look for the first heading.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead:
Regenerate the HTML in `response.html`.

Read the base materials again and use t

risks_fired.evidence: 'Save the updated file.' does not specify overwrite; Claude may pause to ask whether to overwrite response.html or create a new file.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Change 'Save the updated file.' to 'Overwrite `response.html` with the updated version.' to eliminate the overwrite-or-new ambiguity.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead:
Add one small rule to the right file in this folder:
- If the problem is visual

risks_fired.evidence: 'update `styling-rules.md`' and 'update `CLAUDE.md`' use the update verb without naming append-vs-replace boundaries; Claude may replace file contents or append the rule as a disconnected block.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Replace 'update' with 'add one rule to, keeping all existing content' for both file references to name the integration mode explicitly.

risks_fired.evidence: 'regenerate `response.html`' without an explicit overwrite instruction; Claude may confirm before overwriting or silently create response-v2.html.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Overwrite `response.html` with the new version.' inside the fence to close the overwrite ambiguity.

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt has 4+ sub-steps (add rule / read materials / use updated instructions / regenerate response.html / report what changed) with no 'No preamble.' suppressor and no body primer.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'No preamble. Start by adding the rule.' as the final fence line to suppress the multi-step narration.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Then tell me what changed in the file.' asks Claude to self-summarize the delta; body says 'Look at the file again.' but provides no push-back prompt if the summary overstates the improvement.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add a body callout after the fence naming the inflation risk and a push-back prompt: 'Ask Claude which sentence specifically changed if the answer looks the same as before.'
